records.ety: [L. flagellum whip, scourge, in LL., a threshing flail: cf. OF. flael, flaiel, F. flau. See Flagellum.]

defs: An instrument for threshing or beating grain from the ear by hand, consisting of a wooden staff or handle, at the end of which a stouter and shorter pole or club, called a swipe, is so hung as to swing freely.
quotes: His shadowy flail hath threshed the corn.

defs: An ancient military weapon, like the common flail, often having the striking part armed with rows of spikes, or loaded.
quotes: No citizen thought himself safe unless he carried under his coat a small flail, loaded with lead, to brain the Popish assassins.
